Search the internet for arXiv papers by Alejandro Rivero (physicist). Try these searches:
1. Search for "Alejandro Rivero" on arXiv or INSPIRE-HEP
2. Try fetching https://inspirehep.net/search?p=a+A.Rivero.1 or similar INSPIRE queries
3. Try searching for "Alejandro Rivero arxiv physics" 

List all papers you can find with titles, arXiv IDs, years, and co-authors if any. Be thorough.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough search for papers by Alejandro Rivero across multiple sources. Let me run several searches in parallel.

Based on my web search, I found some information about Alejandro Rivero's papers on arXiv:

Alejandro Rivero authored a paper titled "A Short Lecture on Divergences" which presents clues to the study of the renormalization group at graduate level, published in August 2002.

Additionally, Alejandro Rivero from the University of Zaragoza authored "The 115 GeV signal from nuclear physics," which explores coincidences of nuclear mass predictions with high energy physics research, published in July 2004.

The search results show these papers on arXiv, which are indexed in the INSPIRE-HEP system. However, to get a complete and comprehensive list of all of Alejandro Rivero's papers in INSPIRE-HEP, you would need to search directly on the INSPIRE-HEP website using the author's name or INSPIRE author ID (BAI - Basic Author Identifier), as the INSPIRE website interface itself requires JavaScript to display full search results.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Alejandro Rivero's arxiv physics papers:

Alejandro Rivero is affiliated with BIFI at Universidad de Zaragoza, and he has published numerous papers on arxiv spanning several decades.

**Notable Papers:**

1. **Early Work (1994):** With Luis J Boya, he published "Renormalization in 1D Quantum Mechanics: contact interactions," building a toy model of the Wilson-Kogut renormalization group in one dimensional Quantum Mechanics.

2. **Philosophy/History of Science:** He published "Dream of a Christmas lecture" in 2000, which recalled the origins of differential calculus from a modern perspective.

3. **History of Physics:** His paper "Democritus as Taoist" (2003) discussed similarities between tao and Democritus's philosophical system, including chronological parallels and textual parallelism.

4. **Educational Content:** He published "A Short Lecture on Divergences" in 2002, presenting clues to the study of the renormalization group at graduate level.

5. **Recent Work (2024):** His paper "An interpretation of scalars in SO(32)" proposes an interpretation for the adjoint representation of the SO(32) group to classify the scalars of a Supersymmetric Standard Model with three generations.

His research interests appear to span theoretical physics, particularly particle physics and renormalization theory, along with historical and philosophical perspectives on science.

Web search results for query: ""Alejandro Rivero" "Koide" arxiv formula lepton masses"

Links: [{"title":"[hep-ph/0505220] The strange formula of Dr. Koide","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/hep-ph/0505220"},{"title":"arXiv:hep-ph/0505220v1 25 May 2005 The strange formula of Dr. Koide","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/hep-ph/0505220"},{"title":"(PDF) The strange formula of Dr. Koide","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/2015951_The_strange_formula_of_Dr_Koide"},{"title":"[1201.2067] The Koide Lepton Mass Formula and Geometry of Circles","url":"https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/1201.2067"},{"title":"(PDF) The strange formula of Dr. Koide","url":"https://www.academia.edu/70616594/The_strange_formula_of_Dr_Koide"},{"title":"Researchgate","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Alejandro-Rivero-3/publication/275410011_Koide_formula_beyond_charged_leptons/links/553c13410cf29b5ee4b87ff0/Koide-formula-beyond-charged-leptons.pdf"},{"title":"(PDF) Koide formula: beyond charged leptons | Alejandro Rivero - Academia.edu","url":"https://www.academia.edu/33514662/Koide_formula_beyond_charged_leptons"},{"title":"(PDF) The strange formula of Dr. Koide | Alejandro Rivero - Academia.edu","url":"https://www.academia.edu/758668/The_strange_formula_of_Dr_Koide"},{"title":"(PDF) Koide formula: beyond charged leptons","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/275410011_Koide_formula_beyond_charged_leptons"},{"title":"(PDF) The Koide Lepton Mass Formula and Geometry of Circles","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/51988530_The_Koide_Lepton_Mass_Formula_and_Geometry_of_Circles"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Alejandro Rivero's work on the Koide formula:

## Key Findings

Alejandro Rivero from Universidad de Zaragoza, along with co-author Andre Gsponer, published "The strange formula of Dr. Koide," which presents a short historical and bibliographical review of the lepton mass formula of Yoshio Koide, as well as speculations on its extensions to quark and neutrino masses, and its possible relations to more recent theoretical developments.

### Historical Context

At the end of 1981, Yoshio Koide, working on some composite models of quarks and leptons, discovered a very simple relationship between the masses of the three charged leptons, which resulted in a prediction of 1.777 GeV/c² for the mass of the tau lepton.

### Extensions Beyond Leptons

Rivero extended the Koide mass formula to an eigenvector equation, found further coincidences, applied the formula to the neutrinos, and speculatively suggested a complete solution to the problem of the hierarchy of lepton masses and the MNS mixing matrix.

### Geometric Interpretation

Koide's lepton mass formula may be interpreted as a Descartes-like circle formula where the squared curvatures correspond to the masses of the three leptons, and a remarkable formal similarity between Koide's Lepton mass formula and a generalized Descartes circle formula has been reported.

The original paper was published on arXiv as hep-ph/0505220 in May 2005.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found search results related to your query about Alejandro Rivero and his work on supersymmetry and the standard model. Here's a summary of the relevant findings:

Alejandro Rivero is a theoretical physics PhD from Zaragoza University, and he has published extensively on arXiv about supersymmetry and its relationship to the standard model.

Some of his notable papers include:

1. **"Unbroken supersymmetry without new particles"** - This paper considers Koide's equation for charged leptons jointly with hypotheses implied in the ancient Dual Quark-Gluon model, with a focus on motivating the possibility of a supersymmetric framework akin to the one of D=11 maximal supergravity.

2. **"An interpretation of scalars in SO(32)"** - Rivero proposes an interpretation for the adjoint representation of the SO(32) group to classify the scalars of a generic Supersymmetric Standard Model having just three generations of particles, via a flavour group SU(5).

Regarding the "sBootstrap" topic mentioned in your search query, Rivero is a theoretical physics PhD who was too good with computers and too stubborn about research paths, which has led him to explore unconventional approaches to fundamental physics problems.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Papers from InspireHEP Search Results

## Paper 1
**Title:** Ξc(2790)+/0 and Ξc(2815)+/0 radiative decays

**arXiv ID:** 2501.08798

**Year:** 2025

**Authors:** García-Tecocoatzi, H.; Ramirez-Morales, A.; Rivero-Acosta, A.; Santopinto, E.; Vaquera-Araujo, C.A.

**Journal:** Physics Letters B, Volume 868, Article 139666 (2025)

**Summary:** The study examines electromagnetic decay widths of charmed baryons using "constituent quark model formalism" by analyzing P-wave to ground state transitions. Calculations were performed analytically without approximations, showing strong agreement with Belle experimental measurements.

---

## Paper 2
**Title:** An SU(15) approach to bifermion classification

**Year:** 2024

**Authors:** Rivero, Alejandro (with co-authors Corianò, C.; Frampton, P.H.; Melle, D.; Kephart, T.W.; Yuan, T.C.)

**Journal:** European Physical Journal C, Volume 84, Issue 10, Article 1058 (2024)

**Note:** This second paper appears in the database results but represents a different author (Alejandro Rivero from Zaragoza University, distinct from A. Rivero-Acosta who co-authored the first paper).

input.prompt: List the title, all authors, year, and abstract of this paper.
List the title, all authors, year, and abstract of this paper.

content.content:
# Paper Summary

**Title:** "Instanton-like Contributions to the Dynamics of Yang-Mills Fields on the Twisted Torus"

**Authors:** The RTN Collaboration, Garcia Perez, Gonzalez-Arroyo, Martinez, Fernandez, Munoz Sudupe, Ruiz-Lorenzo, Azcoiti, Campos, Ciria, Cruz, Iniguez, Lesmes, Piedrafita, Rivero, Tarancon, Tellez, Badoni, and Pech

**Year:** 1993

**Abstract:** The researchers investigate SU(2) lattice gauge theory in small volumes with specific twist parameters. They examine "periodic instantons of Q=1/2" and work to "determine their free energy and their contribution to the splitting of energy flux sectors."

# Paper Summary

**Title:** The U(1)-Higgs Model: Critical Behaviour in the Confinig-Higgs region

**Authors:** Alonso, Azcoiti, Campos, Ciria, Cruz, Iniguez, lesmes, Piedrafita, Rivero, Tarancon, Badoni, Fernandez, Munoz Sudupe, Ruiz Lorenzo, Gonzalez Arroyo, Martinez, Pech, Tellez

**Year:** 1992 (submitted October 9, 1992; published in Nuclear Physics B in 1993)

**Abstract:** 

The researchers conducted a numerical investigation into the critical properties of a U(1)-Higgs lattice model where the Higgs modulus remains fixed. Their work focused on "the region of small gauge coupling where the Higgs and Confining phases merge." They identified "a first order transition line that ends in a second order point," and through parameter space rotation, they established thermodynamic measures and critical exponents. Their measured results demonstrated critical exponent values "in agreement with the mean field prediction," while providing precise determinations of the critical point location and the first-order line's slope.

Web search results for query: ""Alejandro Rivero" arxiv "hep-lat/9210014" "hep-lat/9302007" U(1) Higgs instanton"

content.content:
# Paper Details

**Title:** "Evidence for radiative generation of lepton masses"

**Authors:** Hans de Vries, Alejandro Rivero

**Year:** 2005 (submitted March 11, 2005)

**Abstract:** The authors present "a fit to the experimental charged lepton masses as coming from radiative corrections in QED."

# Paper Details

**Title:** "Tunneling in asymmetric double well: instanton calculus"

**Author:** A. Rivero

**Year:** 2002

The paper was submitted on September 11, 2002. According to the author's comment, it was originally written in 1994 but discovered later in backups.

# Paper Information

**Title:** Connes' Tangent Groupoid and Strict Quantization

**Authors:** José F. Carinena, Jesús Clemente-Gallardo, Eduardo Follana, José M. Gracia-Bondía, Alejandro Rivero, Joseph C. Varilly

**Year:** 1998 (submitted February 20, 1998; final version published in J. Geom. Phys 32(2), 1999)

**Abstract:** The researchers tackle an unresolved challenge in quantization theory by developing Connes' tangent groupoid method. They demonstrate construction of "a strict, flabby quantization, which is moreover an asymptotic morphism and satisfies the reality and traciality constraints, on any oriented Riemannian manifold." The approach generalizes the Moyal rule and provides an introduction to quantization from Connes' geometric perspective.

content.content:
# Paper Details

**Title:** "An interpretation of scalars in SO(32)"

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero

**Year:** 2024 (submitted July 7, 2024)

**Journal Reference:** Eur. Phys. J. C 84, 1058 (2024)

**Abstract:** The paper proposes a method for interpreting the adjoint representation of the SO(32) group to classify scalars in a three-generation Supersymmetric Standard Model using an SU(5) flavor symmetry. The authors demonstrate this interpretation emerges from a self-consistency requirement for composite scalars, focusing exclusively on color and electric charge while introducing an additional chiral quark with +4/3 charge per generation.

# Paper Details

**Title:** "Introduction to the Tangent Grupoid"

**Author:** Alejandro Rivero (Universidad de Zaragoza, Spain)

**Year:** 1997

The paper was submitted on October 23, 1997, and is cataloged as arXiv:dg-ga/9710026 in the Differential Geometry category.

Based on the web search results, I found relevant information about Alejandro Rivero and his work on "sBootstrap."

Alejandro Rivero is a theoretical physics PhD from Zaragoza University, and he works at BIFI. 

The search results include a discussion of the bootstrap approach developed by Geoffrey Chew in the early sixties as an approach to strong interactions, based on S-matrix theory and the principle of "nuclear democracy", which relates to the "sBootstrap" concept in physics.

His arXiv author page is available at http://arxiv.org/a/rivero_a_1, where various papers by Alejandro Rivero are listed, including work on renormalization, particle physics, and other topics. However, the specific "sBootstrap" paper itself was not directly returned in these search results, though it is referenced in a 2007 blog post on Quantum Diaries that features a guest post by Alejandro Rivero on the topic.

content.content:
# Paper Details

**Title:** Assessing the Bias in Communication Networks Sampled from Twitter

**Authors:** Sandra González-Bailón, Ning Wang, Alejandro Rivero, Javier Borge-Holthoefer, Yamir Moreno

**Year:** 2012 (submitted December 7, 2012)

**Journal Reference:** No traditional journal publication is listed. The paper is archived on arXiv as arXiv:1212.1684 [physics.soc-ph]

**Key Finding:** The researchers discovered that "the search API over-represents the more central users and does not offer an accurate picture of peripheral activity," with greater bias observed in mention networks compared to retweet networks.

# Paper Details

**Title:** Structural and Dynamical Patterns on Online Social Networks: the Spanish May 15th Movement as a case study

**Authors:** Javier Borge-Holthoefer, Alejandro Rivero, Iñigo García, Elisa Cauhé, Alfredo Ferrer, Darío Ferrer, David Francos, David Iñiguez, María Pilar Pérez, Gonzalo Ruiz, Francisco Sanz, Fermín Serrano, Cristina Viñas, Alfonso Tarancón, and Yamir Moreno

**Year:** 2011

**Journal Reference:** PLoS ONE 6(8): e23883, 2011

# Paper Details

**Title:** Locating privileged spreaders on an Online Social Network

**Authors:** Javier Borge-Holthoefer, Alejandro Rivero, Yamir Moreno

**Year:** 2011 (submitted November 17, 2011; revised March 6, 2012)

**Journal Reference:** Physical Review E, Volume 85, Article 066123 (June 19, 2012)

**Summary:** The research examines information diffusion in online social networks by analyzing "who (and why) has privileged spreading capabilities" during social contagion events. The study uses complex network analysis to investigate a political protest episode in Spain from 2011, exploring how network topology enables efficient information spreading among users.

Phys. B (1993) |

---

### Summary by Research Theme

The 42 papers span over three decades (1992--2024) and fall into several thematic clusters:

1. **Lattice Gauge Theory** (1992--1993): Two early collaborative papers with large collaborations (RTN) on U(1)-Higgs models and Yang-Mills instantons on twisted tori.

2. **Noncommutative Geometry / Spectral Triples** (1996--2004): A substantial thread including the NCG model of elementary particles, Connes' tangent groupoid, discrete spectral triples, Poincare duality in discrete spaces, and critical dimension of spectral triples. The groupoid paper with Carinena, Clemente-Gallardo, Follana, Gracia-Bondia, and Varilly is the most collaborative of this series.

3. **Lepton Masses, Koide Formula, and the sBootstrap** (1999--2024): The largest and most distinctive thread. Includes "On Generations" (1999), "The strange formula of Dr. Koide" with Gsponer (2005), "Evidence for radiative generation of lepton masses" with de Vries (2005), multiple papers on supersymmetric composites, the Weinberg/GUT angle, anomaly-driven decays, Koide tuples extended to quarks, the q=4/3 diquark, "Unbroken supersymmetry without new particles" (2009), and culminating in "An interpretation of scalars in SO(32)" published in Eur. Phys. J. C (2024).

4. **Nuclear Physics Connections** (2003--2004): Papers connecting nuclear mass models with Standard Model masses and the 115 GeV Higgs-like signal.

5. **Quantum Mechanics / Mathematical Physics** (1994--2002): Renormalization of contact interactions (with Boya), Feynman formula derivation, instanton calculus, and lessons from numerical analysis.

6. **History and Philosophy of Physics** (2000--2003): "Dream of a Christmas lecture," "Rhythmos, Diathige, Trope," "Democritus as Taoist," and "On the section of a cone."

7. **Network Science / Computational Social Science** (2011--2012): Four papers on online social network dynamics related to the Spanish 15-M movement, with collaborators Borge-Holthoefer, Gonzalez-Bailon, Moreno, and others. Published in Scientific Reports, PLoS ONE, and Physical Review E.

---

- [Google Scholar profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=D9BviOkAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [Academia.edu profile](https://unizar.academia.edu/arivero)
- [ResearchGate profile](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Alejandro-Rivero-3)
- [Guest post on sBootstrap at Quantum Diaries Survivor](https://dorigo.wordpress.com/2007/10/16/guest-post-alejandro-rivero-sbootstrap/)
